OPEN SOURCE SIGNAL — Daily Signal #000
13 May 2026

Today’s signals:

1. 📡 Signal One
Telecom surveillance is moving into the infrastructure layer
Citizen Lab documented telecom-linked surveillance activity involving covert location-tracking operations and mobile-network signalling infrastructure.
Source: Citizen Lab

2. 🛰️ Surveillance Watch
Ad-based geolocation is the dark twin of OSINT
Citizen Lab’s Webloc report links ad-based geolocation products to law-enforcement and intelligence customers.
Source: Citizen Lab

3. 🗺️ Map Room
How to verify damage when premium satellite imagery is limited
Bellingcat tracked reported attacks on Iranian police infrastructure using Sentinel-2, PlanetScope where available, OpenStreetMap, Wikimapia, Google Maps and Overpass Turbo.
Source: Bellingcat

4. 🔎 Platform Watch
Xiaohongshu / RedNote deserves a place in platform OSINT
Bellingcat published a guide to Xiaohongshu / RedNote, emphasizing language-specific search, user IDs, platform conventions and the difference between domestic and international visibility.
Source: Bellingcat

5. 🛡️ Investigator OPSEC
Email threats are shifting into identity attacks
Microsoft’s Q1 2026 email threat report focuses on credential phishing, QR-code phishing, CAPTCHA-gated campaigns, adversary-in-the-middle techniques and shifts after disruption of Tycoon2FA.
Source: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

6. 🌐 Infrastructure Signals
Internet disruptions are becoming conflict signals
Cloudflare Radar’s Q1 2026 review highlights internet disruptions, nationwide shutdowns and attacks affecting cloud infrastructure.
Source: Cloudflare Radar

7. ⚠️ Risk Watch
Dark-web OSINT needs hard ethical boundaries
The OSINT Newsletter issue #105 focuses on dark-web OSINT, including tools, strategies, limitations and ways of connecting dark-web signals to surface-web context.
Source: The OSINT Newsletter

Full issue: https://example.org/issues/open-source-signal-2026-05-13.html
